comment_text.sn12.32:1.2: Kaḷāra the Aristocrat is otherwise unknown.
comment_text.sn12.32:1.4: For Phagguna of the Top-Knot, see note on SN 12.12:2.1.
comment_text.sn12.32:8.5: Read khīṇasmiṁ khīṇam’iti with PTS and BJT, paralleling the common phrase vimuttasmiṁ vimuttam’iti (SN 12.61:6.2). | The syntax here is convoluted: “With the end of that source which is the source of rebirth, I knew regarding that ending, ‘it is ended’.”
comment_text.sn12.32:12.2: The Buddha focuses in on the topic of feelings. The commentary explains this with reference to the Dīghanakhasutta (MN 74), which relates the occasion Sāriputta realized arahantship. The main topic, and the basis for Sāriputta’s insight, was feelings. The Buddha wanted to give Sāriputta the chance to highlight his special domain.
comment_text.sn12.32:14.9: Variants for nānussavanti are from the roots sav (“flow”) and sī (“lie”). Pali often couples verbs and nouns from the same root for emphasis, and this is probably the case here, “defilements don’t defile”.
comment_text.sn12.32:16.2: Pubbe appaṭisaṁviditaṁ is an idiom elsewhere used when visiting someone “without letting them know beforehand” (MN 91:26.4, AN 5.50:4.7).

root_text.sn12.32:1.2: Atha kho kaḷārakhattiyo bhikkhu yenāyasmā sāriputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmatā sāriputtena saddhiṁ sammodi.
root_text.sn12.32:1.3: Sammodanīyaṁ kathaṁ sāraṇīyaṁ vītisāretvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho kaḷārakhattiyo bhikkhu āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn12.32:1.4: “moḷiyaphagguno, āvuso sāriputta, bhikkhu sikkhaṁ paccakkhāya hīnāyāvatto”ti.
root_text.sn12.32:1.5: “Na hi nūna so āyasmā imasmiṁ dhammavinaye assāsamalatthā”ti.
root_text.sn12.32:2.1: “Tena hāyasmā sāriputto imasmiṁ dhammavinaye assāsaṁ patto”ti?
root_text.sn12.32:5.1: Atha kho kaḷārakhattiyo bhikkhu uṭṭhāyāsanā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho kaḷārakhattiyo bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn12.32:5.3: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāmī”ti.
root_text.sn12.32:6.1: Atha kho bhagavā aññataraṁ bhikkhuṁ āmantesi:
root_text.sn12.32:6.2: “ehi tvaṁ, bhikkhu, mama vacanena sāriputtaṁ āmantehi:
root_text.sn12.32:6.4: “Evaṁ, bhante”ti kho so bhikkhu bhagavato paṭissutvā yenāyasmā sāriputto tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn12.32:6.6: “Evaṁ, āvuso”ti kho āyasmā sāriputto tassa bhikkhuno paṭissutvā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinnaṁ kho āyasmantaṁ sāriputtaṁ bhagavā etadavoca:
root_text.sn12.32:6.8: ‘khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyā’ti pajānāmī”ti?
root_text.sn12.32:6.9: “Na kho, bhante, etehi padehi etehi byañjanehi attho vutto”ti.
root_text.sn12.32:6.10: “Yena kenacipi, sāriputta, pariyāyena kulaputto aññaṁ byākaroti, atha kho byākataṁ byākatato daṭṭhabban”ti.
root_text.sn12.32:6.12: ‘na kho, bhante, etehi padehi etehi byañjanehi attho vutto’”ti.
root_text.sn12.32:7.2: ‘kathaṁ jānatā pana tayā, āvuso sāriputta, kathaṁ passatā aññā byākatā—
root_text.sn12.32:7.3: khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī’ti.
root_text.sn12.32:7.4: Evaṁ puṭṭho tvaṁ, sāriputta, kinti byākareyyāsī”ti?
root_text.sn12.32:8.2: ‘kathaṁ jānatā pana tayā, āvuso sāriputta, kathaṁ passatā aññā byākatā—
root_text.sn12.32:8.3: khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī’ti;
root_text.sn12.32:8.5: ‘yaṁnidānā, āvuso, jāti, tassa nidānassa khayā khīṇasmiṁ khīṇāmhīti viditaṁ.
root_text.sn12.32:8.7: khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī’ti.
root_text.sn12.32:9.2: ‘jāti panāvuso sāriputta, kiṁnidānā kiṁsamudayā kiṁjātikā kiṁpabhavā’ti?
root_text.sn12.32:9.3: Evaṁ puṭṭho taṁ, sāriputta, kinti byākareyyāsī”ti?
root_text.sn12.32:9.5: ‘jāti panāvuso sāriputta, kiṁnidānā kiṁsamudayā kiṁjātikā kiṁpabhavā’ti?
root_text.sn12.32:9.7: ‘jāti kho, āvuso, bhavanidānā bhavasamudayā bhavajātikā bhavappabhavā’ti.
root_text.sn12.32:10.2: ‘bhavo panāvuso sāriputta, kiṁnidāno kiṁsamudayo kiṁjātiko kiṁpabhavo’ti?
root_text.sn12.32:10.3: Evaṁ puṭṭho tvaṁ, sāriputta, kinti byākareyyāsī”ti?
root_text.sn12.32:10.5: ‘bhavo panāvuso sāriputta, kiṁnidāno kiṁsamudayo kiṁjātiko kiṁpabhavo’ti?
root_text.sn12.32:10.7: ‘bhavo kho, āvuso, upādānanidāno upādānasamudayo upādānajātiko upādānappabhavo’ti.
root_text.sn12.32:11.3: sace pana taṁ, sāriputta, evaṁ puccheyyuṁ—
root_text.sn12.32:11.4: taṇhā panāvuso sāriputta, kiṁnidānā kiṁsamudayā kiṁjātikā kiṁpabhavā’ti?
root_text.sn12.32:11.5: Evaṁ puṭṭho tvaṁ, sāriputta, kinti byākareyyāsī”ti?
root_text.sn12.32:11.7: ‘taṇhā panāvuso sāriputta, kiṁnidānā kiṁsamudayā kiṁjātikā kiṁpabhavā’ti?
root_text.sn12.32:11.9: ‘taṇhā kho, āvuso, vedanānidānā vedanāsamudayā vedanājātikā vedanāpabhavā’ti.
root_text.sn12.32:12.2: ‘kathaṁ jānato pana te, āvuso sāriputta, kathaṁ passato yā vedanāsu nandī sā na upaṭṭhāsī’ti.
root_text.sn12.32:12.3: Evaṁ puṭṭho tvaṁ, sāriputta, kinti byākareyyāsī”ti?
root_text.sn12.32:12.5: ‘kathaṁ jānato pana te, āvuso sāriputta, kathaṁ passato yā vedanāsu nandī sā na upaṭṭhāsī’ti evaṁ puṭṭhohaṁ, bhante, evaṁ byākareyyaṁ:
root_text.sn12.32:12.8: Sukhā vedanā, dukkhā vedanā, adukkhamasukhā vedanā.
root_text.sn12.32:12.9: Imā kho, āvuso, tisso vedanā aniccā.
root_text.sn12.32:12.11: viditaṁ, yā vedanāsu nandī sā na upaṭṭhāsī’ti.
root_text.sn12.32:13.2: Ayampi kho, sāriputta, pariyāyo, etasseva atthassa saṅkhittena veyyākaraṇāya:
root_text.sn12.32:14.2: ‘kathaṁ vimokkhā pana tayā, āvuso sāriputta, aññā byākatā—
root_text.sn12.32:14.3: khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī’ti?
root_text.sn12.32:14.4: Evaṁ puṭṭho tvaṁ, sāriputta, kinti byākareyyāsī”ti?
root_text.sn12.32:14.6: ‘kathaṁ vimokkhā pana tayā, āvuso sāriputta, aññā byākatā—
root_text.sn12.32:14.7: khīṇā jāti, vusitaṁ brahmacariyaṁ, kataṁ karaṇīyaṁ, nāparaṁ itthattāyāti pajānāmī’ti.
root_text.sn12.32:14.9: ‘ajjhattaṁ vimokkhā khvāhaṁ, āvuso, sabbupādānakkhayā tathā sato viharāmi yathā sataṁ viharantaṁ āsavā nānussavanti, attānañca nāvajānāmī’ti.
root_text.sn12.32:15.2: Ayampi kho sāriputta, pariyāyo etasseva atthassa saṅkhittena veyyākaraṇāya—
root_text.sn12.32:15.3: ye āsavā samaṇena vuttā tesvāhaṁ na kaṅkhāmi, te me pahīnāti na vicikicchāmī”ti.
root_text.sn12.32:15.5: Idaṁ vatvā sugato uṭṭhāyāsanā vihāraṁ pāvisi.
root_text.sn12.32:16.1: Tatra kho āyasmā sāriputto acirapakkantassa bhagavato bhikkhū āmantesi:
root_text.sn12.32:16.2: “pubbe appaṭisaṁviditaṁ maṁ, āvuso, bhagavā paṭhamaṁ pañhaṁ apucchi, tassa me ahosi dandhāyitattaṁ.
root_text.sn12.32:16.3: Yato ca kho me, āvuso, bhagavā paṭhamaṁ pañhaṁ anumodi, tassa mayhaṁ, āvuso, etadahosi—
root_text.sn12.32:16.4: divasañcepi maṁ bhagavā etamatthaṁ puccheyya aññamaññehi padehi aññamaññehi pariyāyehi, divasampāhaṁ bhagavato etamatthaṁ byākareyyaṁ aññamaññehi padehi aññamaññehi pariyāyehi.
root_text.sn12.32:16.5: Rattiñcepi maṁ bhagavā etamatthaṁ puccheyya aññamaññehi padehi aññamaññehi pariyāyehi, rattimpāhaṁ bhagavato etamatthaṁ byākareyyaṁ aññamaññehi padehi aññamaññehi pariyāyehi.
root_text.sn12.32:16.6: Rattindivaṁ cepi maṁ bhagavā etamatthaṁ puccheyya aññamaññehi padehi aññamaññehi pariyāyehi, rattindivampāhaṁ bhagavato etamatthaṁ byākareyyaṁ aññamaññehi padehi aññamaññehi pariyāyehi.
root_text.sn12.32:16.7: Dve rattindivāni cepi maṁ bhagavā etamatthaṁ puccheyya …pe…
root_text.sn12.32:16.9: tīṇi rattindivāni cepi maṁ bhagavā etamatthaṁ puccheyya …pe…
root_text.sn12.32:16.11: cattāri rattindivāni cepi maṁ bhagavā etamatthaṁ puccheyya …pe…
root_text.sn12.32:16.13: pañca rattindivāni cepi maṁ bhagavā etamatthaṁ puccheyya …pe…
root_text.sn12.32:16.15: cha rattindivāni cepi maṁ bhagavā etamatthaṁ puccheyya …pe…
root_text.sn12.32:16.17: satta rattindivāni cepi maṁ bhagavā etamatthaṁ puccheyya aññamaññehi padehi aññamaññehi pariyāyehi, satta rattindivānipāhaṁ bhagavato etamatthaṁ byākareyyaṁ aññamaññehi padehi aññamaññehi pariyāyehī”ti.
root_text.sn12.32:17.1: Atha kho kaḷārakhattiyo bhikkhu uṭṭhāyāsanā yena bhagavā tenupasaṅkami; upasaṅkamitvā bhagavantaṁ abhivādetvā ekamantaṁ nisīdi. Ekamantaṁ nisinno kho kaḷārakhattiyo bhikkhu bhagavantaṁ etadavoca:
root_text.sn12.32:17.3: pubbe appaṭisaṁviditaṁ maṁ, āvuso, bhagavā paṭhamaṁ pañhaṁ apucchi, tassa me ahosi dandhāyitattaṁ.
root_text.sn12.32:17.4: Yato ca kho me, āvuso, bhagavā paṭhamaṁ pañhaṁ anumodi, tassa mayhaṁ, āvuso, etadahosi—
root_text.sn12.32:17.5: divasañcepi maṁ bhagavā etamatthaṁ puccheyya aññamaññehi padehi aññamaññehi pariyāyehi, divasampāhaṁ bhagavato etamatthaṁ byākareyyaṁ aññamaññehi padehi aññamaññehi pariyāyehi;
root_text.sn12.32:17.13: satta rattindivāni cepi maṁ bhagavā etamatthaṁ puccheyya aññamaññehi padehi aññamaññehi pariyāyehi, satta rattindivānipāhaṁ bhagavato etamatthaṁ byākareyyaṁ aññamaññehi padehi aññamaññehi pariyāyehī”ti.
root_text.sn12.32:18.1: “Sā hi, bhikkhu, sāriputtassa dhammadhātu suppaṭividdhā, yassā dhammadhātuyā suppaṭividdhattā divasaṁ cepāhaṁ sāriputtaṁ etamatthaṁ puccheyyaṁ aññamaññehi padehi aññamaññehi pariyāyehi, divasampi me sāriputto etamatthaṁ byākareyya aññamaññehi padehi aññamaññehi pariyāyehi.
root_text.sn12.32:18.2: Rattiṁ cepāhaṁ sāriputtaṁ etamatthaṁ puccheyyaṁ aññamaññehi padehi aññamaññehi pariyāyehi, rattimpi me sāriputto etamatthaṁ byākareyya …pe…
root_text.sn12.32:18.3: rattindivaṁ cepāhaṁ sāriputtaṁ etamatthaṁ puccheyyaṁ, rattindivampi me sāriputto etamatthaṁ byākareyya …
root_text.sn12.32:18.4: dve rattindivāni cepāhaṁ sāriputtaṁ etamatthaṁ puccheyyaṁ, dve rattindivānipi me sāriputto etamatthaṁ byākareyya …
root_text.sn12.32:18.5: tīṇi rattindivāni cepāhaṁ sāriputtaṁ etamatthaṁ puccheyyaṁ, tīṇi rattindivānipi me sāriputto etamatthaṁ byākareyya …
root_text.sn12.32:18.6: cattāri rattindivāni cepāhaṁ sāriputtaṁ etamatthaṁ puccheyyaṁ, cattāri rattindivānipi me sāriputto etamatthaṁ byākareyya …
root_text.sn12.32:18.7: pañca rattindivāni cepāhaṁ sāriputtaṁ etamatthaṁ puccheyyaṁ, pañca rattindivānipi me sāriputto etamatthaṁ byākareyya …
root_text.sn12.32:18.8: cha rattindivāni cepāhaṁ sāriputtaṁ etamatthaṁ puccheyyaṁ, cha rattindivānipi me sāriputto etamatthaṁ byākareyya …
root_text.sn12.32:18.9: satta rattindivāni cepāhaṁ sāriputtaṁ etamatthaṁ puccheyyaṁ aññamaññehi padehi aññamaññehi pariyāyehi, satta rattindivānipi me sāriputto etamatthaṁ byākareyya aññamaññehi padehi aññamaññehi pariyāyehī”ti.

translation_text.sn12.32:1.2: Then the mendicant Kaḷāra the Aristocrat went up to Venerable Sāriputta and exchanged greetings with him.
translation_text.sn12.32:1.3: When the greetings and polite conversation were over, he sat down to one side and said to him,
translation_text.sn12.32:1.4: “Reverend Sāriputta, the mendicant Phagguna of the Top-Knot has resigned the training and returned to a lesser life.”
translation_text.sn12.32:1.5: “That venerable mustn’t have gained any solace in this teaching and training.”
translation_text.sn12.32:2.1: “Well then, has Venerable Sāriputta found found in this teaching and training?”
translation_text.sn12.32:5.1: Then Kaḷāra the Aristocrat went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him,
translation_text.sn12.32:5.3: ‘I understand: “Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.”’”
translation_text.sn12.32:6.1: So the Buddha addressed one of the monks,
translation_text.sn12.32:6.2: “Please, monk, in my name tell Sāriputta that
translation_text.sn12.32:6.4: “Yes, sir,” that monk replied. He went to Sāriputta and said to him,
translation_text.sn12.32:6.6: “Yes, reverend,” replied Sāriputta. He went to the Buddha, bowed, and sat down to one side. The Buddha said to him,
translation_text.sn12.32:6.7: “Sāriputta, is it really true that you have declared enlightenment:
translation_text.sn12.32:6.8: ‘I understand: “Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place”’?”
translation_text.sn12.32:6.9: “Sir, I did not state the matter in these words and phrases.”
translation_text.sn12.32:6.10: “Sāriputta, no matter how a gentleman declares enlightenment, what he has declared should be regarded as such.”
translation_text.sn12.32:6.11: “Sir, did I not also say that
translation_text.sn12.32:6.12: I did not state the matter in these words and phrases?”
translation_text.sn12.32:7.1: “Sāriputta, suppose they were to ask you:
translation_text.sn12.32:7.2: ‘But Reverend Sāriputta, how have you known and seen so that you’ve declared enlightenment:
translation_text.sn12.32:7.3: “I understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”’
translation_text.sn12.32:8.1: “Sir, if they were to ask me this,
translation_text.sn12.32:8.5: ‘Reverends, when the source of rebirth ended, I knew “it is ended”.
translation_text.sn12.32:8.7: I understand: “Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.”’
translation_text.sn12.32:8.8: That’s how I’d answer such a question.”
translation_text.sn12.32:9.1: “But Sāriputta, suppose they were to ask you:
translation_text.sn12.32:9.2: ‘But what is the source, origin, birthplace, and inception of rebirth?’
translation_text.sn12.32:9.4: “Sir, if they were to ask me this,
translation_text.sn12.32:9.7: ‘Continued existence is the source, origin, birthplace, and inception of rebirth.’
translation_text.sn12.32:9.8: That’s how I’d answer such a question.”
translation_text.sn12.32:10.1: “But Sāriputta, suppose they were to ask you:
translation_text.sn12.32:10.2: ‘What is the source of continued existence?’
translation_text.sn12.32:10.4: “Sir, if they were to ask me this,
translation_text.sn12.32:10.7: ‘Grasping is the source of continued existence.’
translation_text.sn12.32:10.8: That’s how I’d answer such a question.”
translation_text.sn12.32:11.1: “But Sāriputta, suppose they were to ask you:
translation_text.sn12.32:11.3: But Sāriputta, suppose they were to ask you:
translation_text.sn12.32:11.6: “Sir, if they were to ask me this,
translation_text.sn12.32:11.10: That’s how I’d answer such a question.”
translation_text.sn12.32:12.1: “But Sāriputta, suppose they were to ask you:
translation_text.sn12.32:12.2: ‘But how have you known and seen so that the relishing of feelings is no longer present?’
translation_text.sn12.32:12.4: “Sir, if they were to ask me this,
translation_text.sn12.32:12.11: When I understood this, the relishing of feelings was no longer present.’
translation_text.sn12.32:12.12: That’s how I’d answer such a question.”
translation_text.sn12.32:13.2: The same point may also be briefly explained in this way:
translation_text.sn12.32:14.1: But Sāriputta, suppose they were to ask you:
translation_text.sn12.32:14.2: ‘But Reverend, how have you been liberated that you declare enlightenment:
translation_text.sn12.32:14.3: “I understand: ‘Rebirth is ended, the spiritual journey has been completed, what had to be done has been done, there is nothing further for this place.’”?’
translation_text.sn12.32:14.5: “Sir, if they were to ask me this,
translation_text.sn12.32:14.9: ‘Because of an inner release with the ending of all grasping, I live mindfully so that defilements don’t defile me and I don’t look down on myself.’
translation_text.sn12.32:14.10: That’s how I’d answer such a question.”
translation_text.sn12.32:15.2: The same point may also be briefly explained in this way:
translation_text.sn12.32:15.3: ‘I have no uncertainty regarding the defilements spoken of by the ascetic. I have no doubt that I’ve given them up.’”
translation_text.sn12.32:15.4: That is what the Buddha said.
translation_text.sn12.32:15.5: When he had spoken, the Holy One got up from his seat and entered his dwelling.
translation_text.sn12.32:16.1: Then soon after the Buddha left, Venerable Sāriputta said to the mendicants,
translation_text.sn12.32:16.2: “Reverends, the Buddha asked me the first question without letting me know beforehand, so I hesitated.
translation_text.sn12.32:16.3: But when the Buddha agreed with my answer, I thought:
translation_text.sn12.32:16.4: ‘If the Buddha were to question me all day on this matter in different words and ways, I could answer all day with different words and ways.
translation_text.sn12.32:16.5: If he were to question me all night,
translation_text.sn12.32:16.17: or seven days and nights, I could answer in different words and ways for seven days and nights.’”
translation_text.sn12.32:17.1: Then Kaḷāra the Aristocrat went up to the Buddha, bowed, sat down to one side, and said to him,
translation_text.sn12.32:17.2: “Sir, Venerable Sāriputta has roared his lion’s roar!”
translation_text.sn12.32:17.3: (And he told the Buddha all that Sāriputta had said.)
translation_text.sn12.32:18.1: “Mendicant, Sāriputta has clearly penetrated the principle of the teachings, so that he could answer any questions I might ask him in different words and ways up to the seventh day and night.”

variant_text.sn12.32:6.9: attho → attho ca (bj, sya-all, km, mr)
